title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Food waste has become a major global problem that (0) serious environmental and social consequences. Approximately one-third of all food produced worldwide is thrown away, while millions of people go hungry. This waste occurs at every (1) of the food supply chain, from farms to supermarkets to our own kitchens. When food ends up in landfills, it produces methane, a greenhouse gas that (2) to climate change. The resources used to grow wasted food, including water, energy, and land, are also lost. Governments and organizations are now taking (3) to address this issue. Many countries have introduced programs to redistribute surplus food to those in (4)., rather than letting it go to waste. Supermarkets are beginning to sell imperfect fruit and vegetables at reduced prices, challenging our (5) of what food should look like. At home, we can all make a difference by planning meals carefully, understanding expiry dates, and being (6) about portion sizes. Composting is another way to reduce the environmental (7) of food waste. Tackling this problem requires effort from everyone, but the (8) for both the planet and society could be enormous.

example.explanation: 'Carries consequences' is the correct collocation to express that something has consequences.

questions.explanation: 'Stage of the supply chain' is the correct collocation to refer to the stages of the supply chain.
'Contributes to' is the correct phrasal verb to express that something contributes to a problem.
'Taking measures' is the correct expression to describe official actions to solve a problem.
'Those in need' is a fixed expression that refers to people in need.
'Expectations of' is the most appropriate collocation to refer to what we expect from something.
'Sensible' means sensible or prudent, appropriate to describe rational decisions about portions.
'Environmental impact' is the standard collocation for the effect on the environment.
'Benefits for' is the correct collocation to refer to the benefits something provides.
